title: chimaphila umbellata subsp. cisatlantica
intro: Chimaphila umbellata subsp. cisatlantica, also known as the Eastern Wintergreen, is a subspecies of the Pygmy Wintergreen plant, native to eastern North America. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional herbal medicine, to treat a variety of ailments, including digestive issues and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Eastern Wintergreen include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Eastern Wintergreen is typically prepared in herbal teas or infusions, and can also be used in tinctures or salves to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
